risks: No managed identity configured on App Service despite 'secure by default with managed identity' being stated as an architecture principle — the app will need stored credentials to access Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB
Azure OpenAI has disableLocalAuth set to false — key-based authentication remains enabled, contrary to WAF security best practices that recommend Entra ID-based auth
Both Azure OpenAI and Cosmos DB have publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled with no VNet isolation or private endpoints
No monitoring or observability resources included (Application Insights, Log Analytics workspace) — WAF Operational Excellence pillar gap
APIM Consumption tier has cold-start latency (~1-2 seconds on first request) which may degrade the real-time Q&A user experience
Single-region deployment (eastus only) with no disaster recovery or multi-region failover strategy
App Service Plan B1 tier does not support deployment slots or autoscale — limits operational agility for production
correctionsRecommended: Add a system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity to the App Service (app-docqa-prod-001) and configure RBAC role assignments for Azure OpenAI (Cognitive Services OpenAI User) and Cosmos DB (Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor)
Set disableLocalAuth to true on the Azure OpenAI resource (oai-docqa-prod-001) to enforce Entra ID-only authentication
Add an Application Insights resource and/or Log Analytics workspace for end-to-end monitoring, diagnostics, and alerting across all resources
Add an Azure Key Vault resource for managing any secrets that cannot use managed identity authentication
Plan for VNet integration on App Service and private endpoints on OpenAI, Cosmos DB, and APIM for production network isolation
Consider upgrading APIM from Consumption to BasicV2 or StandardV2 tier for production workloads with sustained traffic to eliminate cold-start latency and gain VNet integration support
